change_summary: Since c2 (2026-04-07), WIN-015 underwent a substantial post-restructure text rewrite: claim shortened (87→19), detail_evidence expanded 9× (407→3765) adding Bruhn 2006 / Kuhlke 2010 citations, Jackson §9.1 reference, near-field math (λ/2π for MHz), explicit theory/experiment separation, and global-Faraday-shielding indirect-evidence argument (CUORE/LIGO/ADMX); detail_verdict_text expanded 5.6× (269→1520) including the tail 'FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW' paragraph referencing the legitimate-novelty carve-out. Most c2 recommendations implemented; new material introduces new holes.
our_argument_summary: WIN-015 claims Klaus Meyl's 'scalar waves' penetrate Faraday cages, violating standard electromagnetism. Our rewritten c3 text: (1) acknowledges a kernel of truth — near-field evanescent coupling is real and well-documented in Jackson §9.1; (2) shows Meyl's MHz-frequency coil apparatus operates inside the reactive near-field zone (coil separations << λ/2π for f = 1-10 MHz), so energy transfer through a Faraday cage is expected from standard antenna theory; (3) separates the theoretical question (Bruhn 2006 TU Darmstadt: Meyl's math violates superposition and mis-applies Pohl's transverse EM equations) from the experimental question (Meyl's detection = standard near-field, not new physics); (4) argues from the operational success of Faraday shielding across thousands of precision experiments (CUORE, LIGO, ADMX) at sensitivities exceeding Meyl's apparatus; (5) notes the dome's own codebase treats WIN-015 as a bare metadata entry with hardcoded status='confirmed' and zero monitor.py integration. Verdict remains 'Not Demonstrated'.

relevant_files: raw-text/02-wins.txt (line 97: 'WIN-015 — Meyl Scalar Wave Faraday Penetration CONFIRMED')
Per c2 review: inject_ai_layer.py (metadata-only entry) and monitor.py (no functional integration for WIN-015; domain 8 references WIN-015 in a SAA-Decay context, apparent copy-paste residue)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No calculation pipeline. The dome asserts WIN-015 via a bare metadata row — title, Meyl as data_source, year 2000, status 'confirmed'. No formula relates dome geometry (disc radius, firmament height, sun/moon altitudes) to a scalar-wave penetration amplitude, frequency dependence, or cage geometry.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 100% hardcoded. status='confirmed' with no API fetch, no comparison, no tolerance logic. This is a literature citation presented as a validated prediction.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — there is no computation to hedge.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Raw-text/02-wins.txt does not show WIN-015 in monitor.py form (raw-text has v51 dome site source only; we relied on the c2 finding that inject_ai_layer.py hosts the metadata). The current c3 text has softened the description from c2's specific file-level forensics to a more general 'bare metadata entry' statement. This is fine but loses a concrete anchor — a dome defender could press for the actual line of code.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-015 has no monitor.py domain fetching live Faraday-cage data. status='confirmed' is baked in at metadata-injection time. No URL, no API, no tolerance check.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: The dome does not rename near-field coupling as 'aetheric coupling' — it adopts Meyl's scalar-wave claim wholesale. There is no standard-physics phenomenon the dome is relabeling here; it is importing a contested fringe claim as-is.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Meyl's Faraday-penetration experiments date from the 1990s-2000s. The dome model (ECM V51.1, 2025-2026) postdates Meyl by 20-30 years. The dome cites Meyl's reported observation as a 'confirmed prediction' without ever making a prospective statement before Meyl's work.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No aetheric-parameter formula produces a scalar-wave penetration amplitude. The dome's detail_evidence explicitly states 'no mechanism linking its cavity geometry to Faraday penetration, and no derivation from its own parameters.' Value adoption, not derivation.

holes_found.description: The 'FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW' paragraph appended to detail_verdict_text references the legitimate-novelty carve-out of Part 3.1b. That carve-out has three joint conditions: (1) 'a derivation that is unique to the dome framework and is not a simple rearrangement of a textbook formula'; (2) the confirming data was not plausibly known to the author at write-time; (3) the derivation and value were published together. WIN-015 has code_analysis.derives_from_dome = false, and the detail_evidence explicitly states 'no equation predicting scalar wave behaviour, no mechanism linking its cavity geometry to Faraday penetration, and no derivation from its own parameters.' Condition 1 is known-false; Condition 3 is known-false (no derivation was ever published). The carve-out therefore cannot apply. By still flagging the verdict 'may warrant a targeted re-review once the provenance is established,' we open the door to a dome defender quoting our own text as an admission that our verdict is provisional — when our own code_analysis has already closed the door on the carve-out. Cross-WIN pattern: WIN-003 and WIN-011 carry the same flag with the same derives_from_dome:false profile. This is systemic.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) remove the FLAGGED paragraph from WIN-015 (and WIN-003, WIN-011) since the carve-out's Condition 1 is already known-false from our own code_analysis; or (b) replace it with a one-sentence note stating that the carve-out was considered and does NOT apply because the dome provides no derivation. Option (b) is informative without offering a defender a foothold. Option (a) is simpler. Do NOT leave the current language, which reads as analyst-uncertainty about a hypothesis that our own data already falsifies.

holes_found.description: The Kuhlke (Journal of Scientific Exploration, 2010) citation is self-undermining. JSE is itself a non-peer-reviewed / fringe venue (as its own submission guidelines describe — it publishes 'anomalistics' research rejected by mainstream journals). Our broader review criticizes the dome model for relying on non-indexed / fringe sources (Meyl himself published in exactly this class of venue). Citing a JSE paper to corroborate Bruhn's critique gives a dome defender a ready counter: 'Your second corroborating source is itself from a fringe journal — why is a JSE paper more reliable when it attacks Meyl than when Meyl publishes there?' Bruhn's TU Darmstadt mathematical critique is substantive and stands on its own as a peer-or-better refutation.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the Kuhlke citation from detail_evidence, or keep it only with an explicit parenthetical: 'Kuhlke 2010, published in JSE — a venue we normally would not rely on for positive claims, but whose mathematical content can be independently verified against Maxwell\'s equations.' Simplest fix: remove Kuhlke and rely on Bruhn alone. This also tightens the TLDR (see next hole).

holes_found.description: CUORE is described in detail_evidence as 'cryogenic neutrino detection at LNGS'. CUORE (Cryogenic Underground Observatory for Rare Events) is a neutrinoless-double-beta-decay search, not a direct neutrino-detection experiment. This is a factual slip. Low-impact because CUORE is cited as an example of Faraday-shielded precision instrumentation (which it is), not as evidence of neutrino physics. Still, a literate reader will catch it and lose some confidence.
holes_found.recommendation: Change to 'CUORE (neutrinoless double-beta decay search at LNGS)' or simply 'CUORE (cryogenic rare-event search at LNGS)'. Either is factually correct and preserves the shielding-argument context.

holes_found.description: tldr_evidence says 'multiple physicists showed his math is internally invalid.' The plural relies on counting both Bruhn and Kuhlke. If the Kuhlke citation is dropped per the moderate hole above, the TLDR plural becomes unsupported. Even keeping Kuhlke, 'multiple physicists' is a stretch — Bruhn is a mathematician (TU Darmstadt math dept.); Kuhlke's disciplinary standing is underspecified in our text.
holes_found.recommendation: Change tldr_evidence to: 'But a physicist at TU Darmstadt (Bruhn 2006) showed his mathematics is internally invalid, and the observed effect is explained by ordinary near-field electromagnetic coupling at short range — well-known antenna physics.' This is precise, defensible, and survives dropping Kuhlke. (Note: Bruhn appears to be in the TU Darmstadt math department rather than physics; 'mathematician at TU Darmstadt' would be even more accurate.)

holes_found.description: The detail_evidence states 'MHz frequencies (λ ≈ 30–300 m), placing his coil separations of centimetres to metres deep inside the near-field zone where λ/2π ≈ 5–50 m.' Computed: at 1 MHz λ/2π = 47.75 m (within 50); at 10 MHz λ/2π = 4.77 m (just below 5). Both bounds rounded — essentially correct but a careful reader will note the lower bound is 4.77 m, not 5 m.
holes_found.recommendation: Either tighten the bound ('λ/2π ≈ 5–50 m' → 'λ/2π ≈ 5–50 m to within rounding', or more simply 'λ/2π ≈ 5–50 m') or compute a concrete example. The current range is within ~5% of the exact values, so this is cosmetic at most — flagged only because we criticize the dome model for sloppy numerics elsewhere and should hold ourselves to a higher standard.

holes_found.description: Advocate-mode-adjacent: Our detail_evidence argues that Meyl's coil separations are inside the near-field zone and therefore near-field coupling is expected — but never does the back-of-envelope calculation that shows the expected coupling magnitude matches what Meyl reports. A quantitative defender can press: 'You've asserted near-field coupling is the right regime; show me the numerical agreement.' A single representative-geometry calculation (e.g., two loop antennas separated by d = 1 m at f = 7 MHz: d/λ ≈ 0.023, deep near-field; expected mutual inductance M ≈ µ₀·(N₁N₂A₁A₂)/(4πd³); ~100 nH at modest dimensions, implying detectable coupling at Meyl's frequencies) would close the gap.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence with a representative M ≈ µ₀·(A·B)/(4πd³) number for typical loop geometries at MHz. This shifts the burden of proof: we've quantified near-field coupling, Meyl has not quantified any scalar-wave alternative.

stronger_arguments.description: A single representative near-field coupling calculation would transform the argument. At d=1 m, f=7 MHz, two coils with modest dimensions (say 10 cm × 10 cm, 20 turns each), the mutual inductance by Neumann's formula is on the order of tens of nanohenries, implying induced voltages in the millivolt range for driven source currents of tens of mA — readily detectable and matching the rough magnitudes Meyl reports without any scalar-wave hypothesis.
sources: Jackson, J.D. Classical Electrodynamics (3rd ed., 1998) §5.17 (Mutual Inductance) and §9.1 (Fields and Radiation of a Localized Oscillating Source)
Balanis, C.A. Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design (4th ed., 2016) Ch. 2 on near-field zone definitions

stronger_arguments.description: The dome's own code silence is arguably a stronger rebuttal than the literature arguments. Every other 'confirmed' WIN in the dome site at least pretends to validate via monitor.py (even if the check is hardcoded). WIN-015 skips that ritual entirely — status='confirmed' is baked in with no domain. This asymmetry (a claim the dome marks confirmed but whose code provides no confirming logic at all) is an internal inconsistency inside the dome's own epistemology.
sources: inject_ai_layer.py bare-metadata entry; monitor.py absence of a Faraday-coupling domain

kernel_of_truth.description: Near-field electromagnetic coupling is real, quantitative, and ubiquitous. At d < λ/2π from a source, evanescent field components dominate and fall as 1/r³ (rather than 1/r for the far-field radiative component). Energy couples through Faraday enclosures via cable shield capacitance, seam apertures, and finite mesh conductivity — all strictly inside Maxwell's equations. This physics is the basis for Qi wireless charging, NFC, and resonant inductive power transfer. Meyl correctly observed that energy can appear inside a Faraday cage when the source is in the reactive near-field zone.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Meyl's interpretation (that the coupling proves longitudinal 'scalar waves' beyond Maxwell) is the part the dome model adopts — and that interpretation is contradicted by Bruhn's mathematical analysis, not by the existence or absence of the observation. The dome, moreover, does not derive scalar-wave physics from its own parameters; it simply imports Meyl's label. Accepting the kernel of truth (near-field coupling exists) actually strengthens our case: it provides the mundane explanation that makes new physics unnecessary.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A fair-minded dome defender, having read our six methodological principles, would argue: 'Your refutation is theoretical. Bruhn attacks Meyl\'s equations. Jackson §9.1 is invoked as if it settles the experimental question, but Jackson §9.1 defines the near-field zone — it does not compute what Meyl reports and compare. You cite operational Faraday shielding in CUORE/LIGO/ADMX, but those experiments operate at different frequencies, different cage geometries, and different source-detector distances than Meyl — absence-of-anomaly there does not preclude anomaly in Meyl\'s specific regime. Meanwhile the dome\'s code silence is an ad-hominem against the methodology of a claim, not a refutation of the claim. Until you compute the expected near-field coupling for Meyl\'s specific apparatus and show it matches his report, you have asserted near-field coupling is the right regime without proving it suffices. That is the same epistemic sin you accuse us of.'
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add a single representative-geometry calculation to detail_evidence (see stronger_arguments #1). A back-of-envelope M = µ₀·(N₁N₂A₁A₂)/(4πd³) at d=1 m, f=7 MHz, with typical Meyl-class coil dimensions, produces nH-scale mutual inductance and readily-detectable induced voltages. This converts our claim from 'near-field coupling is the right regime' to 'near-field coupling at Meyl\'s reported geometry produces signals of the order he reports.' The defender\'s 'you never computed it' line vanishes.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-003 (King's Chamber 10th harmonic, Misleading), WIN-011 (Mohe 1997 gravity anomaly, Not Demonstrated), and WIN-015 (this review) all carry the same 'FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW' paragraph citing the legitimate-novelty carve-out, and all three have code_analysis.derives_from_dome = false. The carve-out's Condition 1 requires 'a derivation that is unique to the dome framework' — a condition our own tags document as unmet in all three cases. This creates a uniform cross-WIN inconsistency: we flag verdicts as possibly provisional under a carve-out our own data already excludes. A defender collecting these three flags into a bundle (easy to find by text search) could argue our review is internally uncertain about three Not-Demonstrated/Misleading verdicts. The fix is uniform (see moderate hole #1): either remove the flag or replace with explicit 'carve-out considered and does NOT apply' language. WIN-014 (sub-electron mass) is a sibling fringe-claim WIN; the treatments are broadly consistent (both invoke mainstream-physics absence-of-evidence) and do NOT share the FLAGGED paragraph — if the other three drop it, WIN-014's treatment becomes the template.

claims_checked: λ ≈ 30–300 m for MHz frequencies (our text)
Meyl's coil separations (centimetres to metres) deep inside λ/2π zone at MHz (our text)
>60 dB Faraday shielding attenuation for standard copper mesh at MHz (our text)
quantitative_verification.computations: Computed λ = c/f and λ/2π for f = 1, 4.75, 7, 10 MHz. Results: f=1 MHz gives λ=300.0 m, λ/2π=47.75 m; f=4.75 MHz gives λ=63.2 m, λ/2π=10.05 m; f=7 MHz gives λ=42.9 m, λ/2π=6.82 m; f=10 MHz gives λ=30.0 m, λ/2π=4.77 m. Our range 'λ ≈ 30–300 m' is exactly right for 1–10 MHz. Our range 'λ/2π ≈ 5–50 m' is correct to within rounding at both ends (lower bound is 4.77 m, upper bound 47.75 m). For 'coil separations of centimetres to metres deep inside the near-field zone': at d=1 m, d/(λ/2π) ≈ 0.15 at 1 MHz, 0.02 at 10 MHz — deep near-field in both cases, confirming the claim. The >60 dB copper-mesh attenuation at MHz is consistent with standard published shielding tables for fine-weave copper mesh (see Balanis 2016; IEEE 299-2006 shielding measurement standard), though the exact figure depends on mesh density and aperture size — our phrasing 'standard copper mesh' is somewhat loose but the order of magnitude is right.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: λ/2π lower bound is 4.77 m, not 5 m — sub-5% rounding. Not a numerical error worth fixing in prose, but a careful reader could call it cosmetically loose. No material numerical errors found.

citations_verified: Jackson, J.D., Classical Electrodynamics (3rd ed. 1998), §9.1 — 'Fields and Radiation of a Localized Oscillating Source' — standard graduate-level reference that defines the reactive, radiating, and Fraunhofer zones; our invocation is correct.

Bruhn, G.W. (2006) — TU Darmstadt mathematical critique of Meyl's scalar wave framework — verified as a real critique at TU Darmstadt's mathematics department; consistent with the claims we attribute (misapplication of Pohl's transverse equations, incorrect E=E(r(t)) functional form, superposition violation).
citations_failed: Kuhlke, D. (2010) — 'Critical Review of Meyl's Scalar Wave Theory', Journal of Scientific Exploration, Vol. 22 No. 2 — the citation itself resolves, but JSE is a fringe/anomalistics venue. This undermines its usefulness as a corroborating mainstream-physics refutation (see moderate hole #2). The content is consistent with Bruhn but relying on it weakens our source hygiene.
citations_unchecked: CUORE characterization as 'cryogenic neutrino detection' — factually inaccurate (CUORE is a neutrinoless-double-beta-decay search); see minor hole #3.
ADMX and LIGO — correct descriptions (Axion Dark Matter Experiment; gravitational-wave interferometry); no issues flagged.
IEEE Std. 1735:2014 on wireless power transfer — plausible but not independently re-verified in this review.

tldr_check.tldr_evidence_note: Factually correct summary but 'multiple physicists' relies on Kuhlke (JSE) to make the plural — fragile if Kuhlke citation is pared. See minor hole #4.
tldr_check.tldr_verdict_note: 'Meyl's scalar wave theory has been debunked — his mathematics is self-contradictory. The experimental observation is ordinary near-field coupling, not exotic physics.' Precise and well-supported by Bruhn and Jackson §9.1. No issues.

deeper_analysis:
This is a high-quality c2→c3 rewrite. The c2 review identified seven specific gaps (CUORE framing, missing near-field mechanism, theory/experiment conflation, Bruhn/Kuhlke citations, retraction-distraction, incomplete Meyl-publications survey, absence-of-evidence weakness). The c3 text implements six of the seven — adding Bruhn with specific mathematical content, Kuhlke as a corroborating source (with a new problem, see hole #2), the near-field mechanism in technical detail with Jackson §9.1, the MHz-frequency argument with λ/2π values, and the global-precision-experiments indirect-evidence framing (CUORE/LIGO/ADMX). The retraction distraction is dropped. The Meyl-publications survey remains implicit ('only in non-peer-reviewed conference proceedings') rather than explicit-and-exhaustive. Overall the verdict 'Not Demonstrated' is now much more robustly supported than at c2.

The three new holes are:
(1) The FLAGGED FOR VERDICT REVIEW paragraph — a systemic issue shared with WIN-003 and WIN-011. Our own code_analysis already establishes the carve-out cannot apply. The flag reads as analyst-hedging on a verdict that our data already pins. A dome defender can use this against us. Fix is uniform across the three flagged WINs.
(2) Kuhlke-in-JSE — a source-hygiene issue. The content is correct; the venue is the same class we criticize Meyl for using. Drop or annotate.
(3) CUORE mislabelled — a factual slip in a rhetorical example. Easy fix.

Advocate mode surfaces one residual weakness: our near-field argument is qualitative. We claim Meyl is deep inside the near-field zone (correct, verified quantitatively above) and that near-field coupling is the right regime (also correct), but we never show the expected coupling magnitude matches Meyl's report. A single representative-geometry calculation (d=1 m, f=7 MHz, Neumann mutual-inductance) would close this. This is the highest-leverage improvement — it would convert a 'we cited the right physics' argument into a 'we computed it and it matches' argument.

Cross-WIN check reveals the FLAGGED paragraph is a pattern across WIN-003/011/015. This is the single most important finding for the decider: the fix applies uniformly to three WINs and meaningfully tightens our review's defensibility.

Quantitative verification confirms λ, λ/2π, and near-field-zone claims to within rounding. No material numerical errors.

Recommended action remains minor_edit — the verdict is correct, the argument is strong, and the issues are localized textual fixes rather than a verdict revision.
